$comment: One recorded disconfirmation attempt (docs/finding-disconfirmation.md §4): the Step 6b Finding Disconfirmation Pass's machine-parseable record of trying to REFUTE a locked, synthesis-bound finding against the manuscript text. Lives in [Project]_Refutation_Record_[runlabel].md — the pass's ENTIRE write surface (write-scope isolation: the ledger is touched only by the synthesis agent's §6b confidence transcription, cross-checked by refutation-write-scope). `id` must resolve to a locked ledger finding (refutation-coverage). counter_evidence_quotes are verbatim, single-line manuscript-snapshot substrings verified against the snapshot the snapshot_path/snapshot_sha256 pair binds (refutation-evidence; the same binding-field pair apodictic.annotation.v1 carries — A6's uniqueness requirement is deliberately dropped: a counter-evidence quote is evidence, not an anchor, so a span occurring three times is still real counter-evidence). There is deliberately NO severity property: outcomes re-map CONFIDENCE only, per the outcome caps table (survived = never confidence-raising; weakened capped at MEDIUM; refuted = LOW/UNCERTAIN) — severity stays with the Deficit Lock. Open-keyed (closed_keys false in _coverage.json) because the severity-key kill is refutation-write-scope's, enforced against the subset checker's unknown-key tolerance. Validated by scripts/refutation_check.py via scripts/apodictic_artifacts.py.
title: APODICTIC finding-disconfirmation attempt (apodictic.refutation.v1)
